speaker: RODERIGO
text: Tush, never tell me! I take it much unkindly

speaker: RODERIGO
text: That thou, Iago, who hast had my purse

speaker: RODERIGO
text: As if the strings were thine, shouldst know of this.

speaker: IAGO
text: ’Sblood, but you’ll not hear me!

speaker: IAGO
text: If ever I did dream of such a matter,

speaker: IAGO
text: Abhor me.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Thou toldst me thou didst hold him in thy hate.

speaker: IAGO
text: Despise me

speaker: IAGO
text: If I do not. Three great ones of the city,

speaker: IAGO
text: In personal suit to make me his lieutenant,

speaker: IAGO
text: Off-capped to him; and, by the faith of man,

speaker: IAGO
text: I know my price, I am worth no worse a place.

speaker: IAGO
text: But he, as loving his own pride and purposes,

speaker: IAGO
text: Evades them with a bombast circumstance,

speaker: IAGO
text: Horribly stuffed with epithets of war,

speaker: IAGO
text: And in conclusion,

speaker: IAGO
text: Nonsuits my mediators. For “Certes,” says he,

speaker: IAGO
text: “I have already chose my officer.”

speaker: IAGO
text: And what was he?

speaker: IAGO
text: Forsooth, a great arithmetician,

speaker: IAGO
text: One Michael Cassio, a Florentine,

speaker: IAGO
text: A fellow almost damned in a fair wife,

speaker: IAGO
text: That never set a squadron in the field,

speaker: IAGO
text: Nor the division of a battle knows

speaker: IAGO
text: More than a spinster—unless the bookish theoric,

speaker: IAGO
text: Wherein the togèd consuls can propose

speaker: IAGO
text: As masterly as he. Mere prattle without practice

speaker: IAGO
text: Is all his soldiership. But he, sir, had th’ election;

speaker: IAGO
text: And I, of whom his eyes had seen the proof

speaker: IAGO
text: At Rhodes, at Cyprus, and on other grounds

speaker: IAGO
text: Christened and heathen, must be beleed and calmed

speaker: IAGO
text: By debitor and creditor. This countercaster,

speaker: IAGO
text: He, in good time, must his lieutenant be,

speaker: IAGO
text: And I, God bless the mark, his Moorship’s ancient.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: By heaven, I rather would have been his hangman.

speaker: IAGO
text: Why, there’s no remedy. ’Tis the curse of service.

speaker: IAGO
text: Preferment goes by letter and affection,

speaker: IAGO
text: And not by old gradation, where each second

speaker: IAGO
text: Stood heir to th’ first. Now, sir, be judge yourself

speaker: IAGO
text: Whether I in any just term am affined

speaker: IAGO
text: To love the Moor.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I would not follow him, then.

speaker: IAGO
text: O, sir, content you.

speaker: IAGO
text: I follow him to serve my turn upon him.

speaker: IAGO
text: We cannot all be masters, nor all masters

speaker: IAGO
text: Cannot be truly followed. You shall mark

speaker: IAGO
text: Many a duteous and knee-crooking knave

speaker: IAGO
text: That, doting on his own obsequious bondage,

speaker: IAGO
text: Wears out his time, much like his master’s ass,

speaker: IAGO
text: For naught but provender, and when he’s old, cashiered.

speaker: IAGO
text: Whip me such honest knaves! Others there are

speaker: IAGO
text: Who, trimmed in forms and visages of duty,

speaker: IAGO
text: Keep yet their hearts attending on themselves,

speaker: IAGO
text: And, throwing but shows of service on their lords,

speaker: IAGO
text: Do well thrive by them; and when they have lined their coats,

speaker: IAGO
text: Do themselves homage. These fellows have some soul,

speaker: IAGO
text: And such a one do I profess myself. For, sir,

speaker: IAGO
text: It is as sure as you are Roderigo,

speaker: IAGO
text: Were I the Moor I would not be Iago.

speaker: IAGO
text: In following him, I follow but myself.

speaker: IAGO
text: Heaven is my judge, not I for love and duty,

speaker: IAGO
text: But seeming so for my peculiar end.

speaker: IAGO
text: For when my outward action doth demonstrate

speaker: IAGO
text: The native act and figure of my heart

speaker: IAGO
text: In complement extern, ’tis not long after

speaker: IAGO
text: But I will wear my heart upon my sleeve

speaker: IAGO
text: For daws to peck at. I am not what I am.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: What a full fortune does the thick-lips owe

speaker: RODERIGO
text: If he can carry ’t thus!

speaker: IAGO
text: Call up her father.

speaker: IAGO
text: Rouse him. Make after him, poison his delight,

speaker: IAGO
text: Proclaim him in the streets; incense her kinsmen,

speaker: IAGO
text: And, though he in a fertile climate dwell,

speaker: IAGO
text: Plague him with flies. Though that his joy be joy,

speaker: IAGO
text: Yet throw such chances of vexation on ’t

speaker: IAGO
text: As it may lose some color.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Here is her father’s house. I’ll call aloud.

speaker: IAGO
text: Do, with like timorous accent and dire yell

speaker: IAGO
text: As when, by night and negligence, the fire

speaker: IAGO
text: Is spied in populous cities.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: What ho, Brabantio! Signior Brabantio, ho!

speaker: IAGO
text: Awake! What ho, Brabantio! Thieves, thieves!

speaker: IAGO
text: Look to your house, your daughter, and your bags!

speaker: IAGO
text: Thieves, thieves!

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: What is the reason of this terrible summons?

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: What is the matter there?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Signior, is all your family within?

speaker: IAGO
text: Are your doors locked?

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Why, wherefore ask you this?

speaker: IAGO
text: Zounds, sir, you’re robbed. For shame, put on your gown!

speaker: IAGO
text: Your heart is burst. You have lost half your soul.

speaker: IAGO
text: Even now, now, very now, an old black ram

speaker: IAGO
text: Is tupping your white ewe. Arise, arise!

speaker: IAGO
text: Awake the snorting citizens with the bell,

speaker: IAGO
text: Or else the devil will make a grandsire of you.

speaker: IAGO
text: Arise, I say!

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: What, have you lost your wits?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Most reverend signior, do you know my voice?

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Not I. What are you?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: My name is Roderigo.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: The worser welcome.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: I have charged thee not to haunt about my doors.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: In honest plainness thou hast heard me say

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: My daughter is not for thee. And now in madness,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Being full of supper and distemp’ring draughts,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Upon malicious bravery dost thou come

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: To start my quiet.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Sir, sir, sir—

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: But thou must needs be sure

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: My spirit and my place have in them power

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: To make this bitter to thee.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Patience, good sir.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: What tell’st thou me of robbing?

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: This is Venice. My house is not a grange.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Most grave Brabantio,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: In simple and pure soul I come to you—

speaker: IAGO
text: Zounds, sir, you are one of those that will not

speaker: IAGO
text: serve God if the devil bid you. Because we come to

speaker: IAGO
text: do you service and you think we are ruffians, you’ll

speaker: IAGO
text: have your daughter covered with a Barbary horse,

speaker: IAGO
text: you’ll have your nephews neigh to you, you’ll have

speaker: IAGO
text: coursers for cousins and jennets for germans.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: What profane wretch art thou?

speaker: IAGO
text: I am one, sir, that comes to tell you your daughter

speaker: IAGO
text: and the Moor are now making the beast with

speaker: IAGO
text: two backs.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Thou art a villain.

speaker: IAGO
text: You are a senator.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: This thou shalt answer. I know thee, Roderigo.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Sir, I will answer anything. But I beseech you,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: If ’t be your pleasure and most wise consent—

speaker: RODERIGO
text: As partly I find it is—that your fair daughter,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: At this odd-even and dull watch o’ th’ night,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Transported with no worse nor better guard

speaker: RODERIGO
text: But with a knave of common hire, a gondolier,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: To the gross clasps of a lascivious Moor:

speaker: RODERIGO
text: If this be known to you, and your allowance,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: We then have done you bold and saucy wrongs.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: But if you know not this, my manners tell me

speaker: RODERIGO
text: We have your wrong rebuke. Do not believe

speaker: RODERIGO
text: That from the sense of all civility

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I thus would play and trifle with your Reverence.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Your daughter, if you have not given her leave,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I say again, hath made a gross revolt,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Tying her duty, beauty, wit, and fortunes

speaker: RODERIGO
text: In an extravagant and wheeling stranger

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Of here and everywhere. Straight satisfy yourself.

speaker: RODERIGO
text: If she be in her chamber or your house,

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Let loose on me the justice of the state

speaker: RODERIGO
text: For thus deluding you.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Strike on the tinder, ho!

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Give me a taper. Call up all my people.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: This accident is not unlike my dream.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Belief of it oppresses me already.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Light, I say, light!

speaker: IAGO
text: Farewell, for I must leave you.

speaker: IAGO
text: It seems not meet nor wholesome to my place

speaker: IAGO
text: To be producted, as if I stay I shall,

speaker: IAGO
text: Against the Moor. For I do know the state,

speaker: IAGO
text: However this may gall him with some check,

speaker: IAGO
text: Cannot with safety cast him, for he’s embarked

speaker: IAGO
text: With such loud reason to the Cyprus wars,

speaker: IAGO
text: Which even now stands in act, that, for their souls,

speaker: IAGO
text: Another of his fathom they have none

speaker: IAGO
text: To lead their business. In which regard,

speaker: IAGO
text: Though I do hate him as I do hell pains,

speaker: IAGO
text: Yet, for necessity of present life,

speaker: IAGO
text: I must show out a flag and sign of love—

speaker: IAGO
text: Which is indeed but sign. That you shall surely find him,

speaker: IAGO
text: Lead to the Sagittary the raisèd search,

speaker: IAGO
text: And there will I be with him. So, farewell.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: It is too true an evil. Gone she is,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: And what’s to come of my despisèd time

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Is naught but bitterness.—Now, Roderigo,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Where didst thou see her?—O, unhappy girl!—

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: With the Moor, sayst thou?—Who would be a father?—

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: How didst thou know ’twas she?—O, she deceives me

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Past thought!—What said she to you?—Get more tapers.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Raise all my kindred.—Are they married, think you?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Truly, I think they are.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: O heaven! How got she out? O treason of the blood!

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Fathers, from hence trust not your daughters’ minds

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: By what you see them act.—Is there not charms

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: By which the property of youth and maidhood

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: May be abused? Have you not read, Roderigo,

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Of some such thing?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: Yes, sir, I have indeed.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Call up my brother.—O, would you had had her!—

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Some one way, some another.—Do you know

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Where we may apprehend her and the Moor?

speaker: RODERIGO
text: I think I can discover him, if you please

speaker: RODERIGO
text: To get good guard and go along with me.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: Pray you lead on. At every house I’ll call.

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: I may command at most.—Get weapons, ho!

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: And raise some special officers of night.—

speaker: BRABANTIO
text: On, good Roderigo. I will deserve your pains.
